meta.title: Video by jinabaobina
meta.description:
Best KBBQ in NYC?
I’ve never fallen in love with a banchan like this before… to the point where I’m actually trying to recreate it at home. I’m coming for you, Celtuce 😭

📍 Cote Steakhouse @cotekoreansteakhouse 
꽃 🌸

Everything we ordered:

The Butcher’s Feast $82 per person�Comes with banchan, 4 cuts of meat, 2 soups, egg soufflé, and frozen yogurt
Added Japanese wagyu +$32

SO much food. I genuinely wish I could’ve taken leftovers home.
And that was everything. We were completely full by the end.
This is one of those meals that feels like a full experience. The service is attentive and seamless without being overbearing, the space is beautiful and intimate, and the food is just… high quality across the board.

I understand why Cote Korean Steakhouse is Michelin recognized.

#kbbq #nycfood #newyork #newyorkfood #michelin

transcript.transcript: The hardest reservation we made in New York was with this K-Barbecue Spa. Keenan literally hopped on at Resi at 7am on the dock a month before and we still only managed a 930 pm vlog. I went in fully expecting it to be overrated but this ended up being one of the best K-Barbecue experiences I've ever had. The space itself sets the tone. Each table feels like your own private booth. Intimate, dimly lit, and elevated without feeling too formal. We ordered the Butcher's feast, $82 per person, and added the Japanese Wagi for another 32. Before I get into anything else I need to apologize to the staff because I asked for one of the Pantan to be refilled at least 7 times. It's called Celtis. A Chinese vegetable often referred to as Chinese lettuce and it was insanely good. I need to try to make this soon. The eggsufle came out soft and airy. Almost custardy, the kimchi stew was rich and deeply fermented with that perfect balances by Centang that warms you instantly. The soybean paste stew was earthy and comforting, and then the meats. Perfectly marbled cooked right in front of you. Each bite was buttery, tender, and packed with flavor. And what really stood out wasn't just the quality, but how everything came together. The Pantan, the service, the pacing, the attention to detail. It felt like a full experience, not just the meal. Also I didn't realize this until I saw the logo, but it's not pronounced Cote. It's like goat and Korean, meaning flour. For the price, I truly think it's worth it. It's one of those places you go for the experience, the quality and the feeling of it all. I would absolutely come back, definitely worth the hype. Would you go to goat steakhouse?
